preview_text:
The model estimates Mexico as a clear favorite with a 71% win probability, while Czech Republic faces only a 7% chance of victory. This represents a substantial gap between the two sides in the model's assessment, leaving the underdog in a difficult position.

The forecast shape reflects Mexico's advantage but acknowledges meaningful uncertainty. A draw occurs in roughly 23% of the model's outcomes, suggesting the match could remain competitive through regulation. The 22-point gap between Mexico's win probability and Czech Republic's win probability indicates the model sees a tilted contest rather than an open one, though not an overwhelming disparity.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge significantly. The books price Mexico at 49% and Czech Republic at 26%, a roughly 22-point swing toward the underdog compared to the model's assessment. Polymarket reflects similar skepticism of the model's Mexico lean. The dashboard makes no claim about which source correctly calibrates this match; the divergence is material and worth noting.
